title: Set up Clerk authentication with Next.js App Router
summary: Complete Clerk auth integration for Next.js App Router — ClerkProvider, middleware, sign-in/sign-up pages, route protection, and server/client auth hooks.
declaration: I will set up Clerk authentication using @clerk/nextjs for the Next.js App Router. This covers middleware, provider setup, auth pages, and route protection. If you need a different auth provider (NextAuth, Supabase Auth, Auth0), say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set up Clerk Authentication with Next.js App Router ### 1. Pre-flight checks - Verify this is a Next.js 13+ project using the App Router (check for `app/` directory and `next.config.*`). - Check if `src/` directory exists — this determines where `middleware.ts` goes. - Check if any existing auth solution is installed (`next-auth`, `@supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs`, etc.). If so, STOP and ask the user how to handle the conflict. - Verify `package.json` exists. ### 2. Install @clerk/nextjs - Run: `npm install @clerk/nextjs@latest` - Verify installation succeeded by checking `node_modules/@clerk/nextjs/package.json` exists. ### 3. Environment variables - Check if `.env.local` exists. If not, create it. - Add the following keys (DO NOT fill in values — instruct the user to get them from https://dashboard.clerk.com): ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY= CLERK_SECRET_KEY= NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/sign-up NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/ NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_UP_URL=/ ``` - **CRITICAL**: `CLERK_SECRET_KEY` must NEVER have the `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix. It is server-only. Exposing it to the client is a security vulnerability. - Tell the user to paste their keys from the Clerk Dashboard before proceeding. ### 4. ClerkProvider in root layout - Open `app/layout.tsx` (or `src/app/layout.tsx` if using `src/`). - Import `ClerkProvider` from `@clerk/nextjs`. - Wrap the entire `{children}` in ``. It MUST be inside `` and `` tags. - Example: ```tsx import { ClerkProvider } from '@clerk/nextjs' export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ) } ``` - **NOTE**: `ClerkProvider` is a Server Component wrapper. Do NOT add `'use client'` to the root layout just for this. ### 5. Middleware (CRITICAL — most common source of errors) - **File location rules**: - If the project uses `src/` directory: create `src/middleware.ts` - If NO `src/` directory: create `middleware.ts` at the project root (same level as `package.json`) - **NEVER** put middleware inside `app/` directory — Next.js will silently ignore it - **NEVER** put middleware inside `pages/` directory - Write the middleware file: ```typescript import { clerkMiddleware, createRouteMatcher } from '@clerk/nextjs/server' const isPublicRoute = createRouteMatcher([ '/sign-in(.*)', '/sign-up(.*)', '/', '/api/webhooks(.*)', ]) export default clerkMiddleware(async (auth, request) => { if (!isPublicRoute(request)) { await auth.protect() } }) export const config = { matcher: [ // Skip Next.js internals and all static files, unless found in search params '/((?!_next|[^?]*\\.(?:html?|css|js(?!on)|jpe?g|webp|png|gif|svg|ttf|woff2?|ico|csv|docx?|xlsx?|zip|webmanifest)).*)', // Always run for API routes '/(api|trpc)(.*)', ], } ``` - **IMPORTANT**: The `matcher` config is what tells Next.js which routes to run middleware on. Without it, middleware runs on EVERY request including static files, which destroys performance. - **IMPORTANT**: `createRouteMatcher` uses path patterns, NOT glob patterns. Parentheses `(.*)` are regex groups. - After creating the file, verify the path is correct relative to the project root. ### 6. Sign-in and sign-up pages - Create catch-all route for sign-in: `app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx` ```tsx import { SignIn } from '@clerk/nextjs' export default function SignInPage() { return (
) } ``` - Create catch-all route for sign-up: `app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx` ```tsx import { SignUp } from '@clerk/nextjs' export default function SignUpPage() { return (
) } ``` - The `[[...sign-in]]` catch-all is required because Clerk uses multi-step flows with sub-routes. - These paths MUST match `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL` env vars. ### 7. Using auth in server components - In Server Components and Server Actions, use `auth()` from `@clerk/nextjs/server`: ```tsx import { auth } from '@clerk/nextjs/server' export default async function ProtectedPage() { const { userId } = await auth() if (!userId) { // redirect or show unauthorized } return
Protected content for user {userId}
} ``` - `auth()` is async in Clerk v5+. Always `await` it. - For getting the full user object server-side: use `currentUser()` from `@clerk/nextjs/server`. ### 8. Using auth in client components - In Client Components (`'use client'`), use hooks: ```tsx 'use client' import { useAuth, useUser } from '@clerk/nextjs' export function UserProfile() { const { isLoaded, userId, isSignedIn } = useAuth() const { user } = useUser() if (!isLoaded) return
Loading...
if (!isSignedIn) return
Not signed in
return
Hello, {user?.firstName}
} ``` - These hooks ONLY work inside `ClerkProvider`. If you get "useAuth can only be used within ClerkProvider", the provider is missing or not wrapping the component tree. - `useAuth()` for auth state (lightweight). `useUser()` for full user data (heavier). - **UserButton** component for a drop-in profile menu: `import { UserButton } from '@clerk/nextjs'` ### 9. Protecting API routes - For Route Handlers (`app/api/*/route.ts`): ```typescript import { auth } from '@clerk/nextjs/server' import { NextResponse } from 'next/server' export async function GET() { const { userId } = await auth() if (!userId) { return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 }) } // ... handle authenticated request } ``` - The middleware already protects routes not in `isPublicRoute`, but explicit checks in API routes provide defense-in-depth. ### 10. Verify the setup - Start the dev server: `npm run dev` - Visit `http://localhost:3000` — should load without errors. - Visit a protected route — should redirect to `/sign-in`. - Check the terminal for middleware-related errors. - If middleware is not firing, verify: (a) file location is correct, (b) `matcher` config is present, (c) the dev server was restarted after creating `middleware.ts`. ### Common errors & fixes - **Middleware not firing** → Wrong file location. Must be `src/middleware.ts` (with src/) or `./middleware.ts` (without src/). NEVER inside `app/`. - **"ClerkProvider is required"** → Root layout is not wrapping children with ``. - **Hydration errors** → Likely mixing server and client auth. `auth()` is server-only, `useAuth()`/`useUser()` are client-only. Never cross the boundary. - **"CLERK_SECRET_KEY is missing"** → `.env.local` not loaded. Restart dev server. Ensure no typos. - **Sign-in page 404** → The catch-all route structure is wrong. Must be `app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx` (double brackets). - **Infinite redirect loop** → Sign-in/sign-up routes are not in `isPublicRoute`. Add them to `createRouteMatcher`. - **API route returns 401 unexpectedly** → The route path is not in the middleware `matcher`, or it's not in `isPublicRoute` when it should be. - **"Cannot find module @clerk/nextjs/server"** → Old version of @clerk/nextjs. Run `npm install @clerk/nextjs@latest`. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT put `middleware.ts` inside the `app/` directory — Next.js ignores it there. - Do NOT add `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix to `CLERK_SECRET_KEY` — this exposes the secret to the browser. - Do NOT use `useAuth()` or `useUser()` in Server Components — they are client-only hooks. - Do NOT use `auth()` in Client Components — it is server-only. - Do NOT skip the `matcher` config in middleware — it will run on every request including static assets. - Do NOT forget to add sign-in/sign-up routes to `isPublicRoute` — this causes infinite redirect loops. - Do NOT use the deprecated `authMiddleware` — use `clerkMiddleware` (Clerk v5+). - Do NOT hardcode Clerk keys in source code — always use environment variables.
